name: Mid Nodal Junctional Rhythm
identifyBy: P wave hidden within QRS complex - simultaneous atrial and ventricular activation
QRS appears normal width - no P wave visible before or after
Cannot see P wave on surface ECG - atria and ventricles activate simultaneously

measurements.qrsDuration: <120ms (may appear slightly wider due to hidden P)

management: As for junctional rhythm - treat underlying cause
Clinical management identical to other junctional variants
pearls: The P wave is simultaneous with QRS - hidden, not absent
May cause a subtle widening of the QRS or small notch at beginning or end of QRS
Esophageal lead may reveal hidden P wave
Practically indistinguishable from junctional rhythm without P waves on surface ECG
differentials: Junctional rhythm with P in QRS vs absent P

qaNotes: Supraventricular rhythm migrated from v1 components; verify P-wave relationship in editor.
